title: %Error: Value too wide for 32-bits expected in this context
body:
I used verilator in a big project, and got an error: Value too wide for 32-bits expected in this context.
So, i checked it's verilog code, and simplifed an example and still got this error:
`%Error: ../expand_32bits.v:12:44: Value too wide for 32-bits expected in this context 35'h100000990`
`                                : ... note: In instance 'expand_32bits'`
`-node: CONST 0x13958a0 <e1131#> {d12bs} @dt=0x13a6290@(G/w35)  35'h100000990`
`   12 |     vlan <= pkt_data[ { (WIDTH-12), 3'b0 } - 1 -: 16];`
`      |                                            ^`

# example
```verilog
// expand_32bits.v
module expand_32bits
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 320
)
(
    input clk,
    input [2559:0] pkt_data,
    output reg [15:0] vlan
);

always @(posedge clk) begin
    vlan <= pkt_data[ { (WIDTH-12), 3'b0 } - 1 -: 16];
end 

endmodule
```
# command line
`verilator --sc --debug --gdbbt --Wno-fatal ../expand_32bits.v`

# verilator --version
Verilator 5.024 2024-04-05 rev UNKNOWN.REV
I didn't try it with the git master version.

#  OS distribution 
CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)

# May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Yes, please.
